transliteration:
saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṁso yathā kurvanti bhārata |
kuryād vidvāṁs tathāsaktaś cikīrṣur loka-saṅgraham ||
literal_meaning: Attached / in actions / the ignorant / just as / act / O Bharata; / should act / the wise / thus / non-attached / desiring to / hold the world together.
contextual_meaning: The wise perform the same visible actions as the emotionally-invested crowd, but inwardly they are ‘on stage’—role-modeling calm, duty-centered behavior so others can imitate a healthier script without feeling preached at.
psychological_state: Conscious role-enactment: outwardly ordinary, inwardly meta-observing while managing the tension between authentic self and social mirror.
trigger_scenario: A manager must lay off staff; she feels the same grief yet must stay steady to guide remaining employees through the crisis without adding her own panic to theirs.
anchor_text: Do your duty calmly, showing others how to act without clinging.
